ucs.title: [LLM] BlackTech (Palmerworm) DLL search-order hijack — signed third-party binary side-loading unsigned DLL from user-writable path
ucs.description: Hunts the signature loader pattern shared by BlackTech's TSCookie, Flagpro, BendyBear, PLEAD and Waterbear families: a legitimate signed third-party binary (Adobe, iTunes, Trend Micro, Kaspersky, Notepad++, etc.) is dropped into a user-writable directory and side-loads a co-located DLL that hijacks an exported function. This catches their characteristic T1574.001 → T1106 chain regardless of which custom backdoor is delivered.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as process values(Processes.process_hash) as process_hash values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("AcroRd32.exe","AcroBroker.exe","iTunesHelper.exe","iTunes.exe","javaw.exe","jusched.exe","PresentationHost.exe","plugin_host.exe","OneDrive.exe","GoogleUpdate.exe","notepad++.exe","vmtoolsd.exe","TmDbg.exe","coreServiceShell.exe","avp.exe","egui.exe") (Processes.process_path="*\\AppData\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Temp\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\ProgramData\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Users\\Public\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\PerfLogs\\*") NOT (Processes.process_path="*\\Program Files*" OR Processes.process_path="C:\\Windows\\*") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process_path Processes.parent_process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=outer dest process_name [ | tstats summariesonly=t values(Filesystem.file_name) as sibling_dlls values(Filesystem.file_path) as sibling_paths from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_name="*.dll" (Filesystem.file_path="*\\AppData\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Temp\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\ProgramData\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Users\\Public\\*") by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.process_name | rename Filesystem.dest as dest Filesystem.process_name as process_name ] | where isnotnull(sibling_dlls) | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | table firstTime lastTime dest user process_name process_path parent sibling_dlls sibling_paths
ucs.kql:
// BlackTech / Palmerworm DLL side-load — signed third-party loader running from user-writable path AND loading a DLL co-located in that path.
let lookback = 7d;
let _user_writable = dynamic([@"\appdata\", @"\temp\", @"\programdata\", @"\users\public\", @"\perflogs\"]);
let _abused_loaders = dynamic([
    "acrord32.exe","acrobroker.exe","itunes.exe","ituneshelper.exe",
    "javaw.exe","jusched.exe","plugin_host.exe","presentationhost.exe",
    "onedrive.exe","googleupdate.exe","notepad++.exe","vmtoolsd.exe",
    "tmdbg.exe","coreserviceshell.exe","avp.exe","egui.exe",
    "hkcmd.exe","igfxtray.exe","setup.exe","sandboxie.exe"]);
DeviceImageLoadEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
| where tolower(InitiatingProcessFileName) in (_abused_loaders)
| where InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any (_user_writable)
| where FolderPath has_any (_user_writable)
// DLL is dropped next to (or one level under) the abused signed binary — classic search-order hijack
| extend _loaderDir = tolower(InitiatingProcessFolderPath), _dllDir = tolower(FolderPath)
| where _dllDir == _loaderDir or _dllDir startswith _loaderDir
| where FileName endswith ".dll"
| where not(FolderPath startswith @"C:\Program Files") and not(FolderPath startswith @"C:\Windows\")
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
    | where tolower(FileName) in (_abused_loaders)
    | project DeviceId, InitiatingProcessId = ProcessId, ProcessCreationTime, ProcessCommandLine, AccountName, IsInitiatingProcessRemoteSession
  ) on DeviceId, InitiatingProcessId
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          LegitBinary = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          LegitPath   = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          LegitSHA256 = InitiatingProcessSHA256,
          SideLoadDLL = FileName,
          DllPath     = FolderPath,
          DllSHA256   = SHA256,
          ProcessCmd  = ProcessCommandLine,
          IsInitiatingProcessRemoteSession
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// BlackTech / Palmerworm DLL side-load — Sentinel via SecurityEvent 4688 (proxy: signed third-party loader executing from a user-writable path is the high-signal artefact when ImageLoad telemetry isn't available).
let lookback = 7d;
let _abused_loaders = dynamic([
    "acrord32.exe","acrobroker.exe","itunes.exe","ituneshelper.exe",
    "javaw.exe","jusched.exe","plugin_host.exe","presentationhost.exe",
    "onedrive.exe","googleupdate.exe","notepad++.exe","vmtoolsd.exe",
    "tmdbg.exe","coreserviceshell.exe","avp.exe","egui.exe",
    "hkcmd.exe","igfxtray.exe"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| extend _img = tolower(NewProcessName)
| extend _file = tostring(split(_img, "\\")[-1])
| where _file in (_abused_loaders)
| where _img has_any ("\\appdata\\", "\\temp\\", "\\programdata\\", "\\users\\public\\", "\\perflogs\\")
| where not(_img startswith "c:\\program files") and not(_img startswith "c:\\windows\\")
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, LegitBinary = _file,
          LegitPath = NewProcessName, ParentProcessName, CommandLine, TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: BlackTech / Palmerworm Signed-Binary DLL Side-Load From User-Writable Path
id: 8c7c2a4e-5b6f-4f0e-9e9b-7b1c0a3d2b1a
status: experimental
description: Detects BlackTech's hallmark DLL search-order hijack pattern where a legitimately signed third-party binary (Adobe, iTunes, Trend Micro, Kaspersky, Notepad++, etc.) is dropped into a user-writable directory and side-loads a co-located malicious DLL.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0098/
  - https://www.trendmicro.com/en_us/research/17/f/following-trail-blacktech-cyber-espionage-campaigns.html
  - https://symantec-enterprise-blogs.security.com/threat-intelligence/palmerworm-blacktech-espionage-attacks
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/04
tags:
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.t1574.001
  - attack.t1036.002
  - detection.threat_hunting
  - g0098
logsource:
  category: image_load
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_loader:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\AcroRd32.exe'
      - '\AcroBroker.exe'
      - '\iTunes.exe'
      - '\iTunesHelper.exe'
      - '\javaw.exe'
      - '\jusched.exe'
      - '\plugin_host.exe'
      - '\PresentationHost.exe'
      - '\OneDrive.exe'
      - '\GoogleUpdate.exe'
      - '\notepad++.exe'
      - '\vmtoolsd.exe'
      - '\TmDbg.exe'
      - '\coreServiceShell.exe'
      - '\avp.exe'
      - '\egui.exe'
  selection_loader_path:
    Image|contains:
      - '\AppData\'
      - '\Temp\'
      - '\ProgramData\'
      - '\Users\Public\'
      - '\PerfLogs\'
  selection_dll:
    ImageLoaded|endswith: '.dll'
    ImageLoaded|contains:
      - '\AppData\'
      - '\Temp\'
      - '\ProgramData\'
      - '\Users\Public\'
      - '\PerfLogs\'
  filter_signed_paths:
    ImageLoaded|startswith:
      - 'C:\Program Files'
      - 'C:\Windows\'
  condition: selection_loader and selection_loader_path and selection_dll and not filter_signed_paths
falsepositives:
  - Portable apps run from user profiles by power users
  - Software updaters that stage signed binaries in %ProgramData% prior to install
level: high
ucs.rationale: BlackTech (CrowdStrike: PALMERWORM; Microsoft tracks overlap with CIRCUIT PANDA) has used DLL search-order hijacking as the loader for nearly every documented family — TSCookie, Flagpro, BendyBear, PLEAD, Waterbear, IconDown, Kivars. Trend Micro (2017), Symantec (2020) and JPCERT all describe the same pattern: a stolen-cert-signed third-party binary is staged in a user-writable directory together

_field_issues.message: `IsInitiatingProcessRemoteSession` is not a canonical column of DeviceImageLoadEvents, DeviceProcessEvents. Did you mean `InitiatingProcessTokenElevation`?

_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma tag must start with namespace separated with dot from remaining tag.

ucs.title: [LLM] BlackTech SoftEther VPN tunnel staging on internal hosts
ucs.description: Hunts BlackTech's distinctive lateral-movement and persistence pattern flagged by CISA/NSA/JPCERT: deployment of SoftEther VPN client/server binaries (vpncmd.exe, vpnclient.exe, vpnserver.exe, vpnbridge.exe) on compromised endpoints to build covert tunnels — frequently followed by internal port-scanning (T1046) using the same bridge.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmd values(Processes.process_hash) as hash values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("vpncmd.exe","vpnclient.exe","vpnserver.exe","vpnbridge.exe","vpnsmgr.exe") OR (Processes.process_name="cmd.exe" AND Processes.process IN ("*vpncmd*","*AccountCreate*","*NicCreate*","*BridgeCreate*")) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process_path Processes.parent_process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
ucs.kql:
// BlackTech SoftEther VPN tunnel staging — CISA/JPCERT-documented tradecraft.
let lookback = 14d;
let _softether_bins = dynamic(["vpncmd.exe","vpnclient.exe","vpnserver.exe","vpnbridge.exe","vpnsmgr.exe","vpninstall.exe"]);
let _softether_files = DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
    | where ActionType in ("FileCreated", "FileRenamed")
    | where tolower(FileName) in (_softether_bins)
        or (FileName endswith ".exe" and InitiatingProcessVersionInfoCompanyName has "SoftEther")
    | project FileTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, FileName, FolderPath, SHA256,
              InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessAccountName;
let _softether_procs = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where tolower(FileName) in (_softether_bins)
        or ProcessVersionInfoCompanyName has "SoftEther"
        or ProcessVersionInfoProductName has "SoftEther VPN"
        or ProcessCommandLine has_any ("AccountCreate", "NicCreate", "BridgeCreate", "HubCreate", "vpn_client.config", "vpn_server.config")
    | project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, FolderPath,
              ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              IsInitiatingProcessRemoteSession, SHA256;
union isfuzzy=true
    (_softether_files | project Timestamp = FileTime, DeviceName, Source = "FileDrop",
        Detail = strcat(FolderPath, "\\", FileName), SHA256,
        Actor = InitiatingProcessAccountName, By = InitiatingProcessFileName),
    (_softether_procs | project Timestamp, DeviceName, Source = "Execution",
        Detail = ProcessCommandLine, SHA256,
        Actor = AccountName, By = InitiatingProcessFileName)
| summarize FirstSeen = min(Timestamp), LastSeen = max(Timestamp),
            Sources = make_set(Source), Details = make_set(Detail, 32),
            Hashes = make_set(SHA256, 16), Actors = make_set(Actor, 16),
            ParentProcs = make_set(By, 16)
            by DeviceName
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// BlackTech SoftEther VPN tunnel staging — Sentinel SecurityEvent 4688.
let lookback = 14d;
let _softether_bins = dynamic(["vpncmd.exe","vpnclient.exe","vpnserver.exe","vpnbridge.exe","vpnsmgr.exe","vpninstall.exe"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| extend _proc = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| where _proc in (_softether_bins)
    or CommandLine has_any ("AccountCreate", "NicCreate", "BridgeCreate", "HubCreate", "vpn_client.config", "vpn_server.config", "SoftEther")
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, NewProcessName, CommandLine,
          ParentProcessName, TokenElevationType, SubjectUserName
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.rationale: CISA/NSA/FBI/JPCERT joint advisory AA23-270A explicitly named SoftEther VPN abuse as a BlackTech signature for building covert internal tunnels and pivoting between subsidiary and parent networks (their preferred Taiwan→Japan→US route). SoftEther is rare in enterprise estates outside the legitimate vendor — any standalone vpncmd.exe execution by a user-context process is high-fidelity. The conditi

_field_issues.message: `IsInitiatingProcessRemoteSession` is not a canonical column of DeviceFileEvents, DeviceProcessEvents. Did you mean `InitiatingProcessTokenElevation`?
